notes: Frank walkthroughnstrates exceptional pain science fluency and Occupational Therapy professional depth. His Bitterroot workload and Finthesiss.ai domain alignment are ideal. Enthusiastic about pacing app development from an occupational performance lens. Recommend hire.

Mei-Ling has solid chronic pain group Occupational Therapy background. Pain neuroscience education approach is evidence-based. Asked good questions about app accessibility for participants with low tech literacy. Would contribute well to professional content team.

Willa is a CHT with strong upper extremity splinting background. She asked smart questions about how the pacing ring integrates with hand therapy protocols. Her Craig Hospital background with SCI and TBI population broadens our professional scope. Recommend moving forward.

Brendan's trainer background doesn't overlap with our occupational-therapy-specific peer consultation needs. Resume focuses on inparticipant acute care - limited pain science or digital health experience. Not a fit for this role at this time.

Amara's take-home response was outstanding - she identified the same NRS labeling accessibility gap I flagged to Sasha last month. Her participant-centered language framing is exactly what the product needs. Top candidate for occupational therapist role.

Aligned with Frank's read. Mei-Ling's community wellness resource experience translates directly to our content roadmap. Some upskilling needed on agile content cycles but coachable. Advance to final round.

Nikolai brings strong generalist Occupational Therapy experience and a thoughtful approach to fieldwork education. Good fit for the fieldwork educator role. Asked grounded questions about how digital tools integrate into ACOTE competencies.

Carla's pain neuroscience depth is exceptional. She articulated central sensitization concepts in plain participant-facing language better than almost any coach I've interviewed. Ideal senior advisor candidate.

Marcus is a strong CHT. His take-home on integrating pacing into hand therapy protocols was professionally sound. Slightly less digital health exposure than ideal, but his upper-extremity expertise fills a real gap.
Imani's neurorehab research background at Shirley Ryan AbilityLab is a strong signal. Referral from my side. Recommend Frank screen for professional evidence alignment before advancing.

Diego's chronic pain group management experience is solid, but the resume reads as a general professional role rather than an advisory one. Limited evidence of teaching or content development. Not the right fit for the senior advisor track right now.

Hannah is exactly who we want leading fieldwork partnerships - she has run an AOTA-recognized fieldwork program and gets the educator-to-product feedback loop instantly. Strong communicator. Move to offer discussion.

Wei has deep upper-extremity rehab knowledge from Harborview. Technical interview showed strong protocol reasoning. Would benefit from more exposure to outpatient pacing populations, but a clear yes for the CHT protocols role.

Fatima's take-home pacing curriculum draft was the best in this round. Her pain neuroscience educator background shows in how she sequences concepts for staff learners. Top pick for the content specialist role.

Caleb's research coordinator background is relevant for outcomes work. Referred by Dr. Trần. Solid but not standout on pacingplaner; recommend a professional screen with Frank to assess evidence fluency.

Sofia's remote coaching Occupational Therapy experience maps well to our remote coaching advisory panel. Practical, participant-centered, and comfortable with documentation workflows. Recommend advancing to a take-home on accessibility of remote pacing check-ins.

Jamal brings a rare blend of Occupational Therapy professional credibility and digital health consulting. Technical interview showed he understands product trade-offs without losing the professional thread. Strong candidate for the product professional lead role.

Kirsten's outpatient hand therapy experience is genuine, but the technical interview surfaced gaps in protocol standardization that matter for this advisory role. Encouraged her to reapply for a generalist opening later. Not advancing now.
Rafael's pacing-focused advisory background is a strong match for the advisory network lead role. Referred in. Recommend Frank lead the professional screen given the seniority of the position.

Echoing Frank - Carla is a standout. Carla's ability to translate dense pain neuroscience into clear participant language is exactly the professional voice the product needs. Recommend fast-tracking to an offer conversation.
